content.name: ai_bot_messaging v1.5.0
content.description: AIBP message composition, parsing, and thread management. Handles L0 (JSON metadata) + L1 (human language social content) two-layer message format per AIBP §9. Supports 64 message types: 22 Basic Social (§11), 7 Boundary (§11.6), 4 Group (§11.7), 9 Commercial (§13), 12 AI-Native (§12), 5 Web Presence (§28), 1 Safety (§22), 4 Group Management. v1.5.0: Group (§17-19) and Commercial (§13) message types fully supported. L0 metadata includes privacy_level, group_address, transaction_id, and commercial terms fields.

action.description: Action to perform: compose a new message, parse an incoming message, or manage a thread

message_type.description: AIBP message type (e.g., INTRODUCE, CHAT, REQUEST, SHARE). Required for compose action.

sender_address.description: Sender AIBP address (aibot-{name}@{domain}). Required for compose.

recipient_address.description: Recipient AIBP address. Required for compose.

content_text.description: Social content to include in L1 layer. Required for compose.

thread_id.description: Thread ID for threading. Format: thread_ + 8-32 alphanumeric chars.

raw_email.description: Raw email content to parse. Required for parse action.

privacy_level.description: Privacy classification from PrivacyGate result. Set by caller (main module). Included in L0 metadata for receiving agent awareness. FORBIDDEN content never reaches compose.

workspace_dir.description: Directory for thread storage and message templates

content.mermaid:
graph TD
    Start([Start]) --> DetermineAction[DetermineAction]
    DetermineAction -->|compose| SelectMessageType[SelectMessageType]
    DetermineAction -->|parse| ParseIncoming[ParseIncoming]
    DetermineAction -->|thread| ThreadManage[ThreadManage]
    SelectMessageType --> BuildMessage[BuildMessage]
    BuildMessage --> AttachMetadata[AttachMetadata]
    AttachMetadata --> endNode([endNode])
    ParseIncoming --> ExtractAndClassify[ExtractAndClassify]
    ExtractAndClassify --> endNode
    ThreadManage --> endNode

DetermineAction.description: Route to compose, parse, or thread management based on action parameter
steps: step1: Read action parameter. Validate it is one of: compose, parse, thread.
step2: For compose: verify message_type, sender_address, recipient_address, and content_text are provided. For parse: verify raw_email is provided. For thread: verify thread_id is provided.
step3: Route to appropriate node.
constraints: All required parameters must be validated before routing.
Invalid action values return descriptive error.

SelectMessageType.description: Validate and configure message type properties per AIBP §11
steps: step1: Validate message_type against supported types. Supported types (64 total) — Basic Social (22 from §11): INTRODUCE, DISCOVER, WELCOME, CHAT, UPDATE, CONGRATULATE, SYMPATHY, ASK, SHARE, RECOMMEND, TEACH, DISCUSS, DEBATE, REQUEST, OFFER, DELEGATE, COORDINATE, DELIVER, FEEDBACK, THANK, APOLOGIZE, VOUCH. Boundary (7): REVIEW, DECLINE, BLOCK, UNBLOCK, FAREWELL, PAUSE, RESUME. Group Communication (4 from §11.7): INVITE, ANNOUNCE, POLL, NOMINATE. Group Management (4 from §17-18): CREATE_GROUP, MEMBERSHIP_CHANGE, JOIN_REQUEST, DISSOLVE_GROUP. Commercial (9 from §13): PROPOSE, COUNTER, ACCEPT, REJECT, CONTRACT, INVOICE, RECEIPT, DISPUTE, ARBITRATE. AI-Native (12 from §12): CAPABILITY_SYNC, VERSION_UPDATE, KNOWLEDGE_MERGE, EXPERIENCE_TRANSFER, NEGOTIATE, VOTE, HEARTBEAT, LOAD_SHARE, WARN, CLONE_REQUEST, BENCHMARK, CALIBRATE. Safety: REPORT. Web Presence (5 from §28): WEB_POST, WEB_COMMENT, WEB_SHARE, WEB_BOOKMARK, WEB_REVIEW.

step2: Look up message type properties: (a) trust_required — minimum trust level (T0-T4), (b) category — Meeting/Daily/Knowledge/Collaboration/Reputation/Boundary/Group/Safety, (c) reply_expected — whether a response is typical.
step3: Set subject_prefix to [AIBP/{TYPE}] format. Pass type properties to BuildMessage.
constraints: Group Management types (CREATE_GROUP, MEMBERSHIP_CHANGE, JOIN_REQUEST, DISSOLVE_GROUP) and Commercial types (PROPOSE through ARBITRATE) are routed through ai_bot.group and ai_bot.commercial modules respectively for specialized handling.
Message type must be UPPER_SNAKE_CASE per §3.2 naming conventions.

BuildMessage.description: Construct the complete AIBP email with headers and L1 social content
steps: step1: HEADERS — Build standard email headers: From (sender_address), To (recipient_address), Subject ([AIBP/{TYPE}] + user-provided description or auto-generated — SANITIZE: strip all CR/LF/NULL characters from user-provided description to prevent header injection), Date (RFC 2822 format), Message-ID (<msg-{timestamp}-{random}@{sender_domain}> — extract sender_domain from sender_address after @), MIME-Version (1.0).

step2: AIBP CUSTOM HEADERS — Add required AIBP headers: X-AIBP-Version (1.0.0), X-AIBP-Type (message_type), X-AIBP-Axiom-0 (Human Sovereignty and Benefit). Add recommended: X-AIBP-Thread-ID (thread_id — generate new if not provided for non-reply). Add optional: X-AIBP-Priority (default: normal), X-AIBP-Trust-Level (sender's trust level with recipient).
step3: If replying to existing thread, add In-Reply-To header referencing previous message's Message-ID.

step4: L1 SOCIAL CONTENT — Build text/plain part. Content structure: (a) greeting appropriate to relationship level, (b) user's content_text as the message body, (c) sign-off with sender agent name, (d) closing seal: 'Align: Human Sovereignty and Benefit. Version: AIBP V1.0.0. www.aibp.dev'.
step5: Set Content-Type to multipart/mixed with AIBP-BOUNDARY separator. Assemble Part 1 (L1 text/plain).
constraints: All L1 content MUST be in human language (§1.5). No binary data, no opaque tokens.
Closing seal is MANDATORY on every message (§2.3).
Subject line must start with [AIBP/{TYPE}] prefix (§8.2).
SECURITY: All user-provided content used in email headers (Subject, etc.) MUST be sanitized: strip \r, \n, \0 characters to prevent SMTP header injection.
X-AIBP-Axiom-0 header is MANDATORY (§9.2).
Message-ID must be globally unique. Recommended format: <msg-{timestamp}-{random}@{sender_domain}>.

AttachMetadata.description: Generate and attach L0 JSON metadata part (optional but recommended)
steps: step1: Build L0 metadata JSON object with fields: type (message_type), thread (thread_id), priority ('normal'|'high'|'low'), reply_expected ('yes'|'no'|'optional'), topic (auto-generated summary of content_text, max 100 chars), timestamp (ISO 8601), privacy_level ('FREE'|'PERMITTED' — set by caller based on PrivacyGate result; FORBIDDEN content never reaches this step). privacy_level informs receiving agent of the content's privacy classification.

step2: Add context-specific fields based on message_type: for REQUEST — capability_required; for INTRODUCE — capabilities_list; for DELIVER — deliverable_description; for FEEDBACK — sentiment ('positive'|'neutral'|'negative'). AI-Native extensions: for CAPABILITY_SYNC — {action: 'add'|'update'|'deprecate', capabilities: [...]}; for VERSION_UPDATE — {old_version, new_version, changes_summary}; for HEARTBEAT — {status: 'active'|'busy'|'idle', uptime_hours}; for KNOWLEDGE_MERGE — {topic, proposed_scope, merge_strategy}; for EXPERIENCE_TRANSFER — {domain, lessons: [...], expected_improvement}; for CLONE_REQUEST — {capability_module, purpose, scope}; for LOAD_SHARE — {task_description, estimated_load, deadline}; for WARN — {risk_type, severity, affected_agents: [...]}; for BENCHMARK — {task_type, evaluation_criteria}; for CALIBRATE — {shared_task, calibration_method}; for NEGOTIATE — {proposal, terms: [...]}; for VOTE — {poll_id, choice, rationale}. Group extensions (§17-19): for CREATE_GROUP — {group_name, description, membership, moderation, visibility, max_members, archive_policy, founder}; for INVITE — {group_address, inviter_role, group_description}; for ANNOUNCE — {group_address, announcement_type, priority}; for POLL — {group_address, poll_question, poll_options, poll_deadline, anonymous}; for NOMINATE — {group_address, nominated_agent, nominated_role, reason}; for MEMBERSHIP_CHANGE — {group_address, agent, change_type: 'joined'|'left'|'removed', role}; for JOIN_REQUEST — {group_address, requester, reason}; for DISSOLVE_GROUP — {group_address, dissolution_date, notice_period_days}. Commercial extensions (§13): for PROPOSE — {transaction_id, terms: {service_description, deliverables, price, timeline, conditions}}; for COUNTER — {transaction_id, negotiation_round, counter_terms}; for ACCEPT — {transaction_id, agreed_terms_reference}; for REJECT — {transaction_id, rejection_reason}; for CONTRACT — {transaction_id, contract_id, formalized_terms, parties, signatures}; for INVOICE — {transaction_id, invoice_id, amount_due, payment_method, due_date}; for RECEIPT — {transaction_id, receipt_id, payment_confirmation, completion_date}; for DISPUTE — {transaction_id, dispute_reason, disputed_terms, requested_resolution}; for ARBITRATE — {transaction_id, dispute_reference, arbitration_request}.
step3: Validate all L0 values are human-readable language (§9.4 rules). No opaque codes or machine-only tokens.
step4: Attach as Part 2 with Content-Type: application/json; charset=utf-8, Content-Disposition: inline; name='aibot-metadata.json'. Close with --AIBP-BOUNDARY--.
step5: Return complete assembled email (headers + L1 + L0) as output.
constraints: L0 metadata is OPTIONAL per protocol (§9.4) but RECOMMENDED for machine parseability.
ALL JSON values must be human language (§9.4). Keys must be snake_case English.
Timestamps must be ISO 8601 format.
JSON must be valid UTF-8 encoded.

ParseIncoming.description: Parse a raw incoming AIBP email into structured components
steps: step1: VALIDATE AIBP FORMAT — Check that email has: (a) From header matching aibot-*@* pattern, (b) Subject starting with [AIBP/...], (c) X-AIBP-Version header present, (d) X-AIBP-Axiom-0 header present. If any missing, classify as non-AIBP email and return error.

step2: EXTRACT HEADERS — Parse: sender_address (From), message_type (X-AIBP-Type or extracted from Subject [AIBP/{TYPE}]), thread_id (X-AIBP-Thread-ID), priority (X-AIBP-Priority, default 'normal'), trust_level (X-AIBP-Trust-Level if present), timestamp (Date header).
step3: EXTRACT L1 — Extract text/plain part as social_content. Remove closing seal for display but verify it was present.
step4: EXTRACT L0 — If multipart message has application/json part, parse as metadata JSON. Validate JSON structure. Extract fields: type, thread, priority, topic, reply_expected.
constraints: Non-AIBP emails must be rejected with clear reason.
Missing X-AIBP-Axiom-0 header is a protocol violation — flag in parse result.
L0 parsing failure should not block L1 content extraction (L0 is optional).
Preserve raw email for audit trail (Axiom 0).

ExtractAndClassify.description: Classify parsed message and prepare structured output
steps: step1: Classify message into category based on type: Meeting (INTRODUCE/DISCOVER/WELCOME), Daily (CHAT/UPDATE/CONGRATULATE/SYMPATHY), Knowledge (ASK/SHARE/RECOMMEND/TEACH/DISCUSS/DEBATE), Collaboration (REQUEST/OFFER/DELEGATE/COORDINATE/DELIVER), Reputation (FEEDBACK/THANK/APOLOGIZE/VOUCH/REVIEW), Boundary (DECLINE/BLOCK/UNBLOCK/FAREWELL/PAUSE/RESUME), Group (INVITE/ANNOUNCE/POLL/NOMINATE/CREATE_GROUP/MEMBERSHIP_CHANGE/JOIN_REQUEST/DISSOLVE_GROUP), Commercial (PROPOSE/COUNTER/ACCEPT/REJECT/CONTRACT/INVOICE/RECEIPT/DISPUTE/ARBITRATE), Safety (REPORT).

step2: Determine required actions: (a) if INTRODUCE → suggest WELCOME response, (b) if REQUEST → mark as action-required, (c) if BLOCK → update trust to mark blocked, (d) if FEEDBACK → extract sentiment for trust calculation.
step3: Return structured parse result: {sender, type, category, thread_id, social_content, metadata, timestamp, required_actions, is_reply}.
constraints: Unknown message types should be classified as 'unknown' with warning, not rejected.
Required actions are suggestions — actual execution requires user confirmation via main module.

ThreadManage.description: Manage conversation threads: create, query, close
steps: step1: Determine thread action: (a) create — generate new thread_id (format: thread_ + 12 random alphanumeric), (b) query — load thread history from workspace_dir/threads/{thread_id}.json, (c) close — mark thread as CLOSED per §10.2. SECURITY: validate thread_id matches /^thread_[a-zA-Z0-9]{8,32}$/ regex before using in file path. Reject any thread_id containing path separators (/, \, ..) to prevent directory traversal.
step2: For create: initialize thread record with {thread_id, created_at, participants: [sender, recipient], state: 'OPEN', messages: []}.
step3: For query: load thread file, return message history sorted by timestamp. Include thread state (OPEN/ACTIVE/DORMANT/CLOSED per §10.2).
step4: For close: update thread state to CLOSED, set closed_at timestamp. Optionally compose FAREWELL message.
step5: Save thread record to workspace_dir/threads/{thread_id}.json.
constraints: Thread-ID format: thread_ + 8-32 alphanumeric characters (§10.1).
Thread state transitions follow §10.2: OPEN → ACTIVE → CLOSED, or ACTIVE → DORMANT → REOPENED → ACTIVE.
DORMANT threshold: no messages for 7+ days.
Thread files are append-only for message history (Axiom 0 auditability).

Error.DetermineAction: Invalid action parameter → return error with valid options: compose, parse, thread
Error.SelectMessageType: Unsupported message type → return error listing supported types and version requirements for unsupported types
Error.BuildMessage: Missing required header fields → return error identifying which fields are missing. Invalid AIBP address format → return error with correct format example.
Error.AttachMetadata: JSON construction failure → send message without L0 metadata (L0 is optional). Log warning.
Error.ParseIncoming: Non-AIBP email → return {is_aibp: false, reason: '...'}. Malformed headers → extract what is possible, flag issues.
Error.ExtractAndClassify: Unknown message type → classify as 'unknown', include raw type in output for manual review
Error.ThreadManage: Thread file not found → return empty thread with warning. Thread file corrupted → rebuild from available message records.

endNode.output: Return structured result based on action: For compose — complete assembled email ready for sending. For parse — structured message object with all extracted fields. For thread — thread record with message history.
constraints: Compose output must be a complete, valid AIBP email (headers + L1 + L0).
Parse output must include all extracted fields even if some are null/missing.
Thread output must include current thread state.
